data.title: Hong Kong Police Warn Against AI-Based Crypto Investment Scams
data.description: Hong Kong police have revealed a scam involving 'AI quantitative trading' that led to a woman losing approximately HKD 7.7 million. According to Odaily, the fraudsters posed as 'investment experts' and contacted the victim via Telegram, claiming they could achieve stable high returns using 'quantitative trading' and 'AI algorithms.' The victim transferred USDT and ETH worth about HKD 7.7 million to specified addresses over 17 transactions. She realized she had been deceived when her withdrawal attempts were rejected. Hong Kong police caution that while cryptocurrencies have high potential returns, they also come with high volatility and risk. They warn the public to be wary of scams promising high returns, low risk, and low entry barriers.

data.title: Revolut Plans IPO by 2028, Expands in U.S. Market
data.description:
Revolut, Europe's largest fintech company, is planning an initial public offering (IPO) within the next two years, aiming for a listing by 2028. According to ChainCatcher, CEO Nik Storonsky revealed this timeline during an interview with David Rubenstein. Before going public, Revolut intends to provide liquidity for employees and early investors through secondary equity sales, with a new round of secondary transactions potentially starting in 2026. The company's latest valuation stands at $75 billion.

In addition to its IPO plans, Revolut is actively expanding into the U.S. market. The company has completed its second application for a U.S. banking license. If approved, this would allow Revolut to directly access the Federal Reserve's payment system and offer loans and credit card services to American customers.

data.title: BNB Plus Corp. Discloses Dual Developments: Strategic Pivot Review and Nasdaq Compliance Hearing Over Bid Price Deficiency
data.description: BNB Plus Corp. (Nasdaq: BNBX), a digital asset treasury company focused on Binance's BNB token, announces that it has authorized a formal review of strategic alternatives — including merger, reverse merger, acquisition, asset sale, and capital transactions — with initial focus on AI infrastructure, digital infrastructure, defense technologies, and healthcare. As of April 18, the company's treasury held approximately $12.2 million, representing roughly 3.4 times its current market capitalization; BNB Plus has also implemented an active yield-generation strategy on its BNB holdings and retained Lucid Capital Markets as financial advisor. Separately, the company disclosed that on March 20 it received a Nasdaq bid price deficiency notice under Listing Rule 5550(a)(2) and is ineligible for a standard compliance period due to a reverse stock split within the prior year; BNB Plus has requested a hearing before the Nasdaq Hearings Panel, and shares will continue to trade at least through the hearing's conclusion.

data.title: Apple's CEO Transition Maintains Conservative Crypto Stance
data.description: Apple's recent leadership change from Tim Cook to John Ternus has not altered the company's cautious approach to cryptocurrency. According to NS3.AI, the App Store continues to impose a 30% commission on digital goods, including NFTs and crypto-related in-app purchases. A study by Counterpoint Research revealed that 41% of first-time crypto buyers worldwide used Apple Pay to fund their initial purchases, as third-party wallets increasingly adopt the payment method for crypto transactions.

data.title: Empery Digital reduced its holdings by 20 BTC last week, bringing its total Bitcoin holdings down to 2,914 BTC.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin treasury company Empery Digital disclosed that it sold 20 bitcoins last week at an average price of $74,425, generating approximately $1.5 million in revenue. Its total Bitcoin holdings have decreased to 2,914 bitcoins. The company also disclosed that it has spent nearly $150 million to repurchase 26,244,657 shares of common stock and will continue to sell Bitcoin as needed to fund future share buybacks and potentially repay some outstanding debt. (Businesswire)

data.title: Hong Kong police have exposed a cryptocurrency scam involving "AI-powered quantitative trading for guaranteed profits," resulting in losses of HK$7.7 million for victims.
data.description: Hong Kong police have disclosed a fraud case involving a woman who was lured into investing in cryptocurrencies under the guise of "AI quantitative trading." The scammers, posing as "investment experts," contacted the victim via Telegram, claiming they could achieve stable, high returns using "quantitative trading" and "AI algorithms." The victim transferred approximately HK$7.7 million worth of USDT and ETH from her e-wallet to a designated address on 17 separate occasions. She only realized she had been scammed when her attempts to withdraw the funds were refused. Hong Kong police warn that while cryptocurrencies offer high potential returns, they also come with high volatility and high risk. Claims of "AI trading" and "guaranteed profits through quantitative trading" are often scam bait. The public should be wary of the "impossible triangle" trap of high returns, low risk, and low entry barriers. (HK01)

data.title: A major whale/institution that bought the dip in BTC and ETH transferred 2,000 cbBTC to Coinbase this morning, making a profit of $5.37 million.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember's monitoring, the whale/institution that spent $500 million to buy BTC and ETH in early February transferred its last 2,000 cbBTC ($151 million) to Coinbase in the early morning. Its average cost for the 4,000 cbBTC it bought in early February was $73,837, and it has now transferred them all to Coinbase at an average price of $75,181, making a profit of $5.37 million.

data.title: Vercel releases security incident analysis: Third-party AI tool compromised, leading to unauthorized access; no sensitive data alteration found.
data.description: Vercel released a security incident analysis, indicating that some of its internal systems suffered unauthorized access. The incident stemmed from a compromise of Context.ai, a third-party AI tool used by an employee. The attacker used this compromise to take over the employee's Google Workspace account and access some environment configuration data. The initial impact is that a small number of customers may have had their environment variables (such as API keys and tokens) not marked as "sensitive" leaked. Affected users have been notified and advised to immediately rotate their credentials. Currently, there is no evidence that data or supply chains marked as "sensitive" (such as npm packages) have been tampered with. Vercel stated that the attacker possessed a high level of technical skill and has launched an investigation in conjunction with Mandiant and several other security agencies, and has filed a police report. Vercel emphasized that platform services are still operating normally and officially recommends that users enable multi-factor authentication, fully rotate potentially compromised environment variables, and check account activity logs and deployment records to prevent further risks.

data.title: Bezos' AI Labs was valued at nearly $38 billion in its funding round.
data.description: Jeff Bezos is close to completing a $10 billion funding round, valuing his lab at $38 billion. The lab focuses on developing artificial intelligence technologies that can understand the physical world and transform engineering and manufacturing. According to sources familiar with the matter, the company, codenamed "Project Prometheus," plans to close a funding round soon, bringing its valuation to $38 billion, including the new investment. Sources added that this deal will make the company one of the world's most well-funded early-stage startups, including the $6.2 billion initial funding raised in November, but the size of the round has been further increased due to strong market demand. JPMorgan Chase and BlackRock are among the participants in this new round of investment, according to one source. (Financial Times)

data.title: AI TRENDS | Jeff Bezos Nears $10 Billion Funding Deal for AI Lab Valued at $38 Billion
data.description:
Jeff Bezos is on the verge of completing a $10 billion funding deal for his AI-focused laboratory, which is valued at $38 billion. According to Odaily, the lab is dedicated to developing artificial intelligence technologies that can comprehend the physical world and transform engineering and manufacturing sectors.

Insiders reveal that the company, known as 'Project Prometheus,' plans to finalize a funding round soon, raising its valuation to $38 billion with the new capital. The agreement is set to position the company as one of the world's most well-funded early-stage startups, including an initial $6.2 billion raised in November. Due to high market demand, the funding scale has been further expanded. A source indicates that JPMorgan Chase and BlackRock are among the participants in this new investment round.

data.title: Polymarket Predicts Hormuz Strait Normalization by May 31
data.description:
The probability of the Hormuz Strait returning to normal by May 31 has increased to 68%, according to Odaily. This marks a 9% rise over the past 24 hours on Polymarket.

The event contract stipulates that if the IMF Portwatch reports a seven-day moving average of ship arrivals at the Hormuz Strait equal to or above 60 on any date from market creation until May 31, 2026, the market will resolve as 'Yes.' Otherwise, it will resolve as 'No.' The daily count includes container ships, bulk carriers, roll-on/roll-off ships, general cargo ships, and tankers, excluding those not reported by the IMF Portwatch.

U.S. Vice President Vance is scheduled to visit Pakistan on Tuesday for talks regarding Iran. U.S. President Donald Trump, in a recent phone interview, expressed confidence in potential discussions in Pakistan, stating that Iran 'will negotiate.' He warned of unprecedented issues if negotiations do not occur and expressed hope for a 'fair deal,' emphasizing that Iran will not possess nuclear weapons. Trump defended military actions against Iran, stating they were necessary and that the situation would eventually be resolved.

data.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Strait of Hormuz Disruption and U.S.-Iran Tensions
data.description: Renewed disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz and escalating tensions between the United States and Iran have led to an increase in oil prices. According to NS3.AI, these developments have also strengthened the U.S. dollar index and revived concerns about inflation. While silver may experience larger short-term fluctuations compared to gold, the spot-market drivers and industrial demand expectations for silver remain weak.

data.title: Australia to Invest Billions in Anti-Drone Defense Systems Amid Global Security Concerns
data.description: Australia is set to invest billions of dollars in developing anti-drone defense systems, as highlighted by the ongoing conflicts in Iran and Ukraine. Bloomberg posted on X that the increasing use of unmanned vehicles in these regions has emphasized the need for advanced defense mechanisms. This move reflects a broader trend in global security, where nations are prioritizing the development of technologies to counter the growing threat posed by drones. The investment aims to enhance Australia's defense capabilities and ensure preparedness in a rapidly changing security landscape.

data.title: Iran questions the sincerity of the United States and its refusal to negotiate has ulterior motives.
data.description: Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Ghalibaf announced early on the 21st local time that US President Trump, through imposing blockades and violating the ceasefire agreement, is attempting to turn the negotiating table into a surrender table for Iran, or to find an excuse to provoke another war. Currently, the US is frequently announcing plans to send a delegation to the negotiations, while Iran has stated its refusal to negotiate. Analysts point out that Iran's statements reflect multiple considerations and concerns: First, Iran questions the US's sincerity in negotiations. Iran once announced a conditional temporary opening of the Strait of Hormuz, but this did not result in the US lifting its blockade of Iranian ports. Second, it's a negotiation tactic. "Refusing to negotiate" is often a significant bargaining chip outside the negotiating table. If Iran shows an eagerness to negotiate, the US is likely to exert further pressure. In the absence of basic trust, both sides will engage in a series of probing maneuvers before negotiations, testing each other's bottom lines. Third, there are strong voices and anti-American sentiment within Iran. Hardliners believe that Iran's premature statement of returning to the negotiating table under continued US pressure is a sign of compromise and concession. Currently, the US and Iran have disagreements on multiple issues, including the nuclear issue, passage through the Strait of Hormuz, and sanctions against Iran. There is a serious lack of mutual trust between the two sides, and a significant gap exists between their expectations for achieving these goals through negotiations. Analysis indicates that the current situation may unfold in several ways: First, both sides may return to the negotiating table within the ceasefire period, or reach a consensus to extend the ceasefire and continue negotiations. However, the likelihood of reaching a comprehensive and long-term agreement in the short term is low, and negotiations could break down again at any time due to a hardline statement or action from one side. Second, the two sides may descend into a "limited-scale conflict." After the ceasefire expires, the military standoff and harassment between the US and Iran around the Strait of Hormuz will continue, and hostilities may reignite. Third, the conflict could escalate on a large scale and spiral out of control. The US has recently continued to issue military threats against Iran, but US analysts believe that due to rising war costs and increased electoral political pressure, the Trump administration has relatively limited decision-making space for a large-scale escalation of the conflict. (CCTV)

data.title: Planet Morning News
data.description:
1. Trump: The new agreement will be much better than the Iran nuclear deal; the ceasefire is "extremely unlikely" to be extended.
2. Trend Research, owned by Yi Lihua, transferred 5.41 million UNI and 228,000 COMP to a new address.
3. Warsh's leaked testimony sparks controversy; the hearing may not be as high-profile as usual.
4. Analyst: Spark's current USDC deposit pool TVL is $467 million, with Justin Sun's funds accounting for nearly 22.5%.
5. A whale withdrew 18,300 ETH from Binance, of which 12,000 ETH were sold to repay Aave loans.
6. Bloomberg analyst: Bitcoin ETF inflows have exceeded $1 billion this year.
7. DefiLlama founder: Three possible scenarios for handling the Kelp security incident; the worst-case scenario could lead to over $300 million in bad debts for Aave.
8. Claude Desktop was exposed for secretly writing browser backdoor files.
9. OCBC Bank of Singapore plans to launch physical gold fund tokens on the Ethereum and Solana chains.
10. A certain whale sold 497.73 ETH and bought 3.845 billion ASTEROID in the past two days.

data.title: Dune: Nearly half of LayerZero OApps have used a 1-of-1 DVN configuration in the past 90 days.
data.description: Following the KelpDAO attack, Dune published an analysis on its X platform of the DVN security configurations of all active OApps on LayerZero over the past 90 days. The data showed that out of approximately 2,665 individual contracts, about 47% used the lowest security level 1-of-1 DVN configuration, 45% used 2-of-2, and about 5% used 3-of-3 or higher configurations. The analysis concluded that KelpDAO's rsETH falls into the 1-of-1 configuration category, highlighting the widespread inadequacy of current cross-chain applications in terms of security redundancy design.

data.title: Curve founder criticizes frequent vulnerabilities in DeFi: single points of failure pose a hidden danger, calls for unified security standards.
data.description: Curve founder Michael Egorov stated in an article on the X platform that DeFi will remain the future of the global financial system, but recent security incidents, many of which could have been avoided (due to centralized single points of failure), are severely damaging the industry's development. He cited the Aave and rsETH incidents as examples, pointing out that while multiple parties claimed the "system was operating normally," users were unable to withdraw funds, exposing infrastructure dependence and issues of dispersed responsibility. Egorov emphasized that the industry should mitigate single points of failure risk beforehand and disperse trust when unavoidable, while sharing best practices and security experiences. He called for the establishment of unified DeFi security standards and suggested that ecosystem organizations, including the Ethereum Foundation and the Solana Foundation, take the lead in developing principles and standards for building and verifying security.

data.title: North Huachuang Addresses Pricing Strategy Amid Rising Raw Material Costs
data.description: North Huachuang announced on April 21 via its interactive platform that the company sets prices based on market and customer demand. According to Jin10, in response to rising raw material costs, North Huachuang plans to mitigate cost pressures through supply chain optimization and technological innovation. The company currently does not anticipate any price increases.

data.title: Hong Kong Stocks Open Higher with Gains in Hang Seng Index
data.description: Hong Kong's stock market opened with positive momentum as the Hang Seng Index rose by 0.31% at the start of trading. According to Jin10, the Hang Seng Tech Index also saw an increase, opening up by 0.53%. These early gains indicate a positive start for the market, reflecting investor optimism in the region.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 199,999 HYPE from a CEX and sold them for 8.15 million USDC.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet withdrew 199,999 HYPE from a CEX and sold them for 8.15 million USDC. The wallet then withdrew the funds from Hyperliquid and deposited them into Binance.
